stories.title: Charli xcx's Music, Fashion, Film is not a continuation of Brat, but a counterweight
stories.summary: Charli xcx’s new album Music, Fashion, Film marks a bold shift from her 2024 hit Brat, blending rock and hyperpop with 11 genre-defying tracks. The project, co-produced with A.G. Cook and Finn Keane, challenges listeners with raw artistry and playful irony. Critics call it a counterweight to her earlier work, expanding her creative vision beyond club anthems.

stories.title: Phoenix Cyclops revealed as MARVEL Tōkon: Fighting Souls First Year 1 DLC character
stories.summary: Phoenix Cyclops has been confirmed as the first year one DLC character for MARVEL Tōkon: Fighting Souls, a fighting game from Arc System Works, set to join the game in fall 2026. This version of Cyclops draws inspiration from his Phoenix Five incarnation, sporting a bold new look infused with the fiery cosmic power of the Phoenix Force. He will bring devastating Optic Blasts and the cosmic flames of the Phoenix Force to the game, marking a significant addition to the roster.

stories.title: The Game Takes a Bottle to the Head at 'VERZUZ' With YG
stories.summary: The Game stunned fans at Thursday’s VERZUZ clash with YG by downing nearly a full bottle of tequila in one gulp during their West Coast hip-hop showdown. The high-energy event aired live on Apple Music and YouTube, celebrating two decades of Compton rap from both artists. Both rappers traded hits and hometown pride in a night that felt more like a celebration than a competition.

stories.title: Brush fire near S Olympia St forces evacuations in south Kennewick
stories.summary: A brush fire near South Olympia Street in south Kennewick has forced evacuations, with residents in the area of West 50th and South being told to leave immediately. The fire, which broke out on Thursday evening, has spread rapidly and is currently uncontained, with a Level 3 Go Now evacuation order in place. Evacuation shelters have been set up at the South Hills Church parking lot and the Southridge Sports Complex.

stories.title: ‘Dungeon Crawler Carl’ TV Series Casts Audiobook Star Jeff Hays as Princess Donut’s Voice
stories.summary: A live-action TV series adaptation of Matt Dinniman's best-selling LitRPG novels, "Dungeon Crawler Carl," has cast Jeff Hays as the voice of Princess Donut, a key character in the books. Hays, the narrator and producer of the audiobook versions, will bring the character to life in the Peacock TV series. The show, produced by Seth MacFarlane's Fuzzy Door Productions, is set to premiere on Peacock, following an alien invasion that forces survivors to fight for their lives.

stories.title: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: The US has imposed new tariffs ranging from 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, the EU, Canada, Japan and India. The duties, covering most American imports, take effect after a temporary global levy expired following a Supreme Court ruling. The move escalates trade tensions amid claims the targeted nations have failed to address forced labour concerns.

stories.title: What's working - and what's not - for WSL fans?
stories.summary: Attendance at Arsenal Women’s matches soared to an average of 34,000 last season, nearly four times higher than any other WSL club and surpassing eight Premier League teams. A YouGov study in January 2026 found 28.5 million global WSL fans, evenly split by gender with nearly half aged 18-34. Clubs now face the challenge of sustaining growth while improving the supporter experience across the league.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: Young people in coastal towns across England and Wales face growing isolation and limited opportunities, with youth service funding down 73% in 12 years and nearly a million aged 16–24 not in work, education or training. A Guardian project found decades of neglect and negative stereotypes have left many despondent, with few signs of immediate change.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
stories.summary: India’s ‘Cockroach’ Janta Party will hold talks with the government on Friday after 26 days of hunger strike by activist Sonam Wangchuk ended last night. Tens of thousands of young protesters across India demand education minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation over exam paper leaks. The government faces mounting pressure after police crackdowns left scores injured and sparked nationwide outrage.

stories.title: ‘A small snippet’: what pubs make of Burnham’s £100m rates giveaway
stories.summary: Prime Minister Andy Burnham has announced a 20% cut in business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues, costing £100m. The move aims to support struggling high streets amid a trend of closures averaging one per day.

stories.title: Socceroos star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
stories.summary: Australia midfielder Cristian Volpato, 22, tested positive for cocaine after police twice stopped him for speeding in Sydney on Thursday night. He was caught driving between 86-94km/h and later 109km/h in a 60km/h zone, leading to a six-month driving ban. The Socceroos star, who played in three World Cup matches, faces further police inquiries.

stories.title: Volkswagen profits slump as it flags full-year revenue squeeze ahead of restructuring
stories.summary: Volkswagen’s second-quarter profit fell 10% to 3.5 billion euros as the German automaker scrapped its 2026 sales growth forecast, now expecting a 3% revenue decline. The company also announced plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs as part of a sweeping restructuring amid rising costs and fierce competition from Chinese rivals.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron, native to Africa, Asia, the Americas and parts of Europe, has been spotted at the National Trust’s Holnicote Estate near Minehead, Somerset. The sighting follows conservation efforts to restore freshwater habitats that now support water voles, otters, pine martens and white-tailed eagles. Conservationists say the UK has lost over 97% of its wetlands, making such habitats vital for wildlife recovery.

stories.title: The two days which could make or break Fury-AJ
stories.summary: Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua could face off later this year after both win their warm-up fights. Fury faces Mariusz Wach in Thailand on Friday, while Joshua meets Kristian Prenga in Saudi Arabia the next day. If both prevail, they’ll meet in a long-awaited British boxing showdown.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: In England, a unique play area has been created at the Winchester Science Centre to educate children about the country's rare chalk streams, with over 80% of the world's chalk streams found there. The play zone, themed on the local landscape, aims to foster a connection between children and the environment, which they can carry into adulthood. The centre's exhibit highlights the importance of water flow, temperature, and oxygen for these rare freshwater habitats, and how they can be protected.

stories.title: My generation shouldn’t be scared of a care tax – we’re paying one already | Gaby Hinsliff
stories.summary: Generation X now faces the dual burden of caring for aging parents while raising their own children, with many struggling to balance full-time work and long-distance caregiving. A new care tax could ease this strain, but experts warn political hesitation risks shifting the financial and emotional toll onto struggling families. The issue is expected to dominate upcoming policy debates as demand for social care rises.

stories.title: Urgent need for more security after physical attacks and abuse, say MPs’ staff
stories.summary: MPs’ staff are demanding urgent security upgrades after a survey found 78% received threats and nearly one-third fear for their safety. Prime Minister Andy Burnham will announce a review following the murder of former minister Ann Widdecombe, 75, in South Yorkshire, where Joshua Kerry, 28, has been charged. Staff report rising racist abuse and pressure to ignore safety risks.

stories.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
stories.summary: Female veterans’ support groups are gaining national traction as former RAF medic Chelley Tulloch reveals how PTSD from multiple war-zone deployments left her desperate for help. Tulloch now coordinates nationwide networks for women veterans, warning that rising female military recruitment will demand more dedicated mental health services. The Ministry of Defence says it is increasing NHS support for veterans.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study found replacing gas stoves with electric cut hospital visits and missed school or work by 70% for poorly controlled asthma patients. The project, involving 1,200 Cleveland-area households, was halted early in December 2024 after funding ended. Researchers reported asthma improvements matching or exceeding those from common medications.

stories.title: A Million-Panel Project
stories.summary: Utah’s new Green River Energy Center, featuring nearly one million solar panels and 500 batteries, went online in June 2026. The 400-megawatt solar and 400-megawatt battery facility, located 130 miles southeast of Salt Lake City, is among the largest in the region. It will supply power to Salt Lake City and other parts of Utah.

stories.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
stories.summary: Sia’s 2016 concert film Nostalgic for the Present showcases her iconic anthems like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" through a faceless performance that amplifies themes of strength and feminine triumph. The film captures her voice as the sole focal point, symbolizing power beyond the individual. Critics highlight how her abstract presence turns her music into a universal anthem of resilience.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

stories.title: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for teen girl's family
stories.summary: A 21-year-old singer, David Anthony Burke, has been accused of murdering 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez in a gruesome crime that has left her family traumatized. Prosecutors presented graphic crime scene photos in court, showing Celeste's dismembered body, and testified that Burke had ordered online items including a body bag, chainsaws, and a shovel in the days following her death. The hearing is a preliminary step towards a trial, where prosecutors aim to convince the judge that there is sufficient evidence to proceed.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.

stories.title: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
stories.summary: Yemen’s Houthi rebels have attacked Red Sea shipping, forcing vessels to reroute and threatening the main Europe-Asia trade lane. US Central Command has carried out 13 straight nights of strikes on Iranian targets in response to the escalation. Gulf Arab states now face heightened security risks amid rising regional tensions.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: Indonesian babies trafficked to Singapore are now at the center of a legal and humanitarian debate after a court convicted 19 people for selling at least 34 infants, with 12 sent to Singapore. Adoptive parents in Singapore face uncertainty over whether the children will remain with them or return to Indonesia as authorities in both countries deliberate on next steps.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Union for unfairly favouring its own apps over rivals, the first major penalty under the bloc’s new Digital Markets Act. The EU ruled Google’s practices restricted consumer choice and gave its services an unfair advantage, while Google warned the decision could harm services used by millions. The fine combines two separate breaches of the DMA, with the larger penalty imposed after Google was found to have promoted its own services in search results.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.
